title: Preserving Original Order in Duplicate Removal

statement.markdown:
Given a singly linked list with head node `L0`, remove all duplicate elements while preserving the original order of elements.
The linked list is not sorted and may contain circular references.
Return the head node of the modified linked list.
statement.inputFormat: Input is given as a string of space-separated integers where each integer represents a node's value, or a string of space-separated integers in the format `n1 value1 n2 value2 ... nk vk` where each pair represents a node with value `vk` and the previous node has index `n2 - 1`
statement.outputFormat: Return the head node of the modified linked list in the format `1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> None`
statement.notes: Avoid using extra space for iteration or recursion.

hints.text: Use a Set to keep track of encountered elements
Implement a recursive function or iterative solution to traverse the linked list

editorial.title: Preserving Original Order in Duplicate Removal
editorial.intuition: Use a Set to track unique elements, implement a recursive function or iterative solution
editorial.bruteForce: Use a HashSet to keep track of unique elements and a linked list to store result
editorial.betterSolution: Use a recursive function and a Set to avoid duplicate elements
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement an iterative solution with a Set and a linked list to store result
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Verify that the modified linked list is correct and duplicate-free
editorial.dryRun: Test the solution on an example input
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Inefficient use of extra space
Mistake 2: Incorrect implementation of recursive function or iterative solution
